LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS SATISFIED HER INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION BY SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY RESPONSES WERE DEVOID OF ANY FACTUAL INFORMATION SHOWING EITHER BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY OR DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION WERE FILED LESS THAN 14 DAYS PRECEDING THE HEARING AND NO COURT ORDER WAS SOUGHT OR OBTAINED PERMITTING PLAINTIFF TO FILE LATE PAPERS, NOR HAS ANY GOOD CAUSE BEEN SHOWN WHY PLAINTIFF FILED LATE PAPERS. THUS, PER THE MANDATORY PROVISION OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 437C(B)(2), PLAINTIFF'S LATE-FILED PAPERS WERE NOT CONSIDERED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963; DEPT. 302 (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-10-2015 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-27-2015 Set For Case Management Conference On Jul-29-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JAMES K. HOLDER of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel No. (415) 986-5900 appearing for Defendant Shauna Matlin;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SHAUNA MATLIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant's moving papers satisfied her initial burden of production by showing that plaintiff's discovery responses were devoid of any factual information showing either breach of fiduciary duty or damages. Plaintiff's opposition were filed less than 14 days preceding the hearing and no court order was sought or obtained permitting plaintiff to file late papers, nor has any good cause been shown why plaintiff filed late papers. Thus, per the mandatory provision of Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(b)(2), plaintiff's late-filed papers were not considered. (Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal. App. 4th 614, 623). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motoin To Compe Discovery Responses Establish Admissions, And Impose Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-26-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David C. Macpherson, of Law Offices of David C. Macpherson, Tel. 415.924.1235, for plaintiffs and opposing party;
James K. Holder, of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel. 415.986.5900, for defendants and moving party;
No other appearances.

ARGUED. DEFENDANT SHAUNA MATLIN Motion To Compel Discovery Responses Establish Admissions, And Impose Sanctions is denied in part. ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Peter VanZandt; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
